issues.display_title: Issue #1367: Migrate List Log Files
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /logs/files
Current code: LogController@listLogFiles
Why it's simple: directory scan + map

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Extracted listLogFiles from LogController into the ADR pattern. No DTO — the action receives user_id as a plain string and throws a generic Exception if it is empty. The controller resolves user_id using the legacy lookup order: user_data.user_id first, then top-level user_id from the request. The action delegates to LogFileService::listLogFiles().

Response shape is non-standard: { success: true, files: [...] } on success and { success: false, message } on error — not the standard domain envelope.

New files:

  • app/Http/Controllers/Log/ListLogFilesController.php
  • app/Actions/Log/ListLogFilesAction.php

API Docs: docs/v2/log/list-log-files.md

issues.display_title: Issue #1366: Migrate Store Log
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: POST /logs/store
Current code: LogController@store
Why it's simple: passthrough to ElasticLogClass

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Extracted store from LogController into the ADR pattern. No DTO was introduced — the action receives the raw request payload as an array and forwards it directly to ElasticLogService::addLog(). The service wraps the legacy ElasticLogClass to decouple the action layer from the class-based infrastructure.

New files:

  • app/Http/Controllers/Log/StoreLogController.php
  • app/Actions/Log/StoreLogAction.php
  • app/Services/Log/ElasticLogService.php

API Docs: docs/v2/log/store-log.md

issues.display_title: Issue #1363: Migrate Search Address
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: GET /tours/search-address
Current code: SearchAddress@search
Why it's simple: 1 search call + transform

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Extracted search from SearchAddress controller into the ADR pattern. SearchAddressDto carries search (query string) and a hasSearch flag to distinguish a missing key from an empty string — the action throws SearchAddressValidationException if the search key is absent entirely.

This endpoint has no auth middleware (public). The action delegates to ToursLegacyService::searchPlaceV1(). Response shape is non-standard (preserved from legacy): { status, message, data } instead of the standard { statusCode, data: { messages } } envelope.

New files:

  • app/Http/Controllers/Tours/SearchAddressController.php
  • app/Actions/Tours/SearchAddressAction.php
  • app/DTOs/Tours/SearchAddressDto.php
  • app/Exceptions/Tours/SearchAddressValidationException.php

API Docs: docs/v2/tours/search-address.md

issues.display_title: Issue #1314: Migrate Delete Street View Tour
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: DELETE /sv/delete-tour
Current code: Tours\SVPublisherController@deleteTour
Why it's simple: ownership check + CURL POST

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Extracted deleteTour from SVPublisherController into the ADR pattern. DeleteTourDto carries userId and moderationId. The action enforces ownership via ToursRepository::checkModerationOwnership() before calling StreetViewPublisherService::deleteTour(). Expects the response body to be 'Tour deletion completed.'; throws TourDeleteFailedException otherwise.

New files:

  • app/Http/Controllers/Tours/DeleteTourController.php
  • app/Actions/Tours/DeleteTourAction.php
  • app/DTOs/Tours/DeleteTourDto.php
  • app/Exceptions/Tours/TourDeleteFailedException.php
  • app/Exceptions/Tours/ModerationNotOwnerException.php

API Docs: docs/v2/street-view/delete-tour.md

issues.display_title: Issue #1317: Migrate Delete Street View Photo
issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Endpoint: DELETE /sv/delete-photo
Current code: Tours\SVPublisherController@deletePhoto
Why it's simple: CURL DELETE + conditional update

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Extracted deletePhoto from SVPublisherController into the ADR pattern. DeletePhotoDto carries gothruPanoId. The action preserves the legacy fallback behavior: if the upstream moderation API returns HTTP 500, it clears the 5_pano_id column in the panoramas table via StreetViewPublisherRepository::clearPanoId() instead of throwing an error. On any other non-success response, throws PhotoDeleteFailedException.

New files:

  • app/Http/Controllers/Tours/DeletePhotoController.php
  • app/Actions/Tours/DeletePhotoAction.php
  • app/DTOs/Tours/DeletePhotoDto.php
  • app/Repositories/Tours/StreetViewPublisherRepository.php
  • app/Exceptions/Tours/PhotoDeleteFailedException.php

API Docs: docs/v2/street-view/delete-photo.md

issues.main_description_html:
Migrate this endpoint to the Domain-Based ADR architecture.

Why it's simple: CURL with 3-branch parse

Category A — thin logic currently written inline in a large controller. Extract into Controller → Action → Repository with a DTO and exception mapping.

Reference: docs/ADR_MIGRATION_CANDIDATES.md

Note from Developer

Extracted getBluelineStatus from SVPublisherController into the ADR pattern. GetBluelineStatusDto carries the moderationId from the route. The action handles the original 3-branch response logic: success (status 200 with upload time + processing state), no blueline recorded, and missing sequence ID. Throws BluelineStatusFailedException on any unexpected response.

New files:

  • app/Http/Controllers/Tours/GetBluelineStatusController.php
  • app/Actions/Tours/GetBluelineStatusAction.php
  • app/DTOs/Tours/GetBluelineStatusDto.php

  • New repo method ToursRepository::getModerationIdByCid.
  • Removed legacy claimTourStatus; token checks replaced by auth middleware.
  • Tests: ClaimTourStatusActionTest (4 cases, green). PR #1439.

Doc: docs/v2/tours/claim-tour-status.md
